text.title: The Five-Server Threshold and the Schema Bloat Cliff
text.summary: Past 5 MCP servers, flat composition tanks selection accuracy; gateway is mandatory above the threshold.

section.title: The threshold rule

rule.statement: If an agent connects to more than 5 MCP servers OR sees more than 25 tools in its catalog, route through a gateway. Below those thresholds, connect directly. Measure tool count from the agent's perspective — sum of unique tools across all connected servers, after any client-side filtering.
rule.rationale: Tool selection accuracy degrades sharply past ~25 flat tools (Anthropic, Composio guidance). 6 average MCP servers × ~10 tools each crosses both thresholds simultaneously. The gateway lets you lazy-load toolkits and trim the catalog per client without rewriting upstream servers.

section.reference: Composio — tool catalog sizing field guide.
Anthropic — writing effective tools for AI agents.

list.item: Auth: one OAuth perimeter instead of N per-server flows.
Rate limiting: per-tenant + per-tool quotas at one place.
Audit: single append-only log across all upstream calls.
Namespace collision resolution: gateway prefixes upstream tool names (`github_search` vs `linear_search`).
Lazy toolkit loading: agent loads only the toolkit relevant to the task; schemas not in the catalog do not cost tokens.

example.code: # 7 upstream servers behind a gateway; agent sees one gateway: upstreams: [github, linear, sentry, jira, gitlab, slack, notion] toolkits: issue_tracking: [linear.create_ticket, jira.create_issue, github.create_issue] code_search: [github.search_code, gitlab.search_code] incidents: [sentry.search_events, slack.post_to_channel] loaded_per_agent: 1 # agent loads exactly one toolkit per task
example.note: Agent's tool catalog stays under 5 tools per turn; selection accuracy preserved; switch toolkit by task class.

example.code: # Flat connection: client connects to 7 MCP servers, all tools live in system prompt client.mcp_servers = [github, linear, sentry, jira, gitlab, slack, notion] # 70+ tool schemas, ~5k tokens per turn, accuracy collapses
example.note: Past the threshold; cost per turn rises and the model picks wrong tools more often.

antipattern.description: Adding a gateway in front of 2 servers "for future-proofing".
antipattern.reason: Below the threshold the gateway is pure latency and one more thing that can break. Add it when the threshold is crossed, not preemptively.

antipattern.description: Treating the gateway as a security boundary while letting agents bypass it via direct MCP connections.
antipattern.reason: If clients can dial servers directly, the gateway's deny rules are decorative. Enforce gateway-only access at the network layer.
